positions.catalyst: Momentum breakout — +39% on 21M volume (avg much lower). Earth observation/defense satellite theme. Analyst price target $5.50 (Strong Buy consensus). Clean chart move through resistance.
positions.notes: Most tradeable name on today's gainer list. Real company, real revenue (up 37% YoY), institutional-grade ticker. Riding satellite/space defense momentum. Will trail stop up if it holds $5.

positions.catalyst: +73% today on 63M volume vs $21M market cap. Abnormal volume = likely FDA catalyst or partnership news. Lung cancer diagnostics play. High risk — smaller position.
positions.notes: Volume is 3x the entire float equivalent — something is happening here. Taking a half-size position due to risk profile. Stop is tight. If news is confirmed, I'll add. If it fades hard by 2 PM, I exit immediately — these can give back 50% in an hour. NOTE: If this long is stopped out at open March 24, BIAF immediately becomes a SHORT candidate (see short watchlist below).

positions.activateCondition: Only enter SHORT if: (1) BIAF long is closed/stopped out at open, AND (2) price fails to hold $4.00 by 9:50 AM CDT with declining volume
positions.stopLossNote: Stop at entry +3% (e.g., if short at $4.30, stop at $4.43). HARD STOP — no exceptions.
positions.catalyst: FADE SETUP: BIAF ran +73% on March 23 on unconfirmed catalyst. $21M market cap, thinly traded. Pattern: stocks that spike 50–100% on unclear news retrace 30–60% the following session once FOMO buyers exit and no follow-through news materializes. Volume exhaustion likely.
positions.shortThesis: Classic 'gap-up on weak/unconfirmed news' fade. If catalyst is NOT confirmed as material (FDA clearance, partnership contract, etc.) before open March 24, sellers will dominate. Retail momentum buyers from March 23 will be underwater and forced out. No institutional support at these levels.
positions.riskNote: This is a HIGH-RISK short due to: (1) small float = potential squeeze, (2) if real news breaks, this could spike further. Hence CONDITIONAL entry only — must see price failure first. Half-size max.
positions.notes: Do NOT short BIAF if: (a) still holding the long, (b) fresh positive news drops premarket, (c) price gaps DOWN more than 20% at open (risk/reward gone). This only works as a controlled fade entry, not a chase.

positions.activateCondition: Enter SHORT if CHGG bounces to $0.65–0.75 intraday on low volume (dead-cat bounce into resistance)
positions.stopLossNote: Stop at $0.78 (3% above $0.757 max entry). HARD STOP.
positions.catalyst: STRUCTURAL SHORT: Chegg is a broken education-tech business. Revenue down 20%+ YoY. AI (ChatGPT, Claude, etc.) destroyed their tutoring/homework-help moat. Balance sheet deteriorating. Stock has shed 95%+ from all-time highs. Any bounce is a gift — the company is on a slow-bleed path toward delisting or restructuring.
positions.shortThesis: Negative catalyst backdrop: AI disruption is permanent and accelerating. No realistic path back to profitability at current trajectory. The +27% bounce on March 23 was retail FOMO, not institutional re-rating. Smart money is still short or exiting. Bounce into resistance = shorting opportunity.
positions.riskNote: Risk: This stock is cheap enough that a short squeeze on ANY positive news (buyout rumor, debt restructuring deal) could cause +50%+ spike. Keep position ≤20% and stop HARD at $0.78. Do NOT add to this short.
positions.notes: This is the 'slow bleeder' short. Not a day trade in the pure sense — more of a fade after a dead-cat bounce. Exit by 3:30 PM CDT regardless. No overnights without a confirmed hard stop in place.

positions.activateCondition: Enter SHORT only on a bounce — wait for CNXC to rally back to $30.50–$31.50 resistance on declining volume after the initial gap-down. Do NOT short into the hole (already -13.4%).
positions.entryTimeTarget: 10:00–11:30 AM CDT — watch for dead-cat bounce into resistance
positions.stopLossNote: Stop at $32.50 (~3% above $31.50 max entry). HARD STOP. If it reclaims $32.50, the breakdown failed and I'm wrong — get out.
positions.catalyst: BREAKDOWN FADE: CNXC is the biggest laggard today — down 13.44% to $28.60 on heavy volume (123K vs normal). This is almost certainly an earnings/guidance disaster or major negative catalyst. Gap-down breakdowns of this magnitude almost always see a dead-cat bounce in the 10–11 AM window before sellers re-assert control. That bounce is the short entry.
positions.shortThesis: Business process outsourcing (BPO) sector already under AI pressure. A -13% single-day gap down signals institutional distribution — fund managers unloading, not rotating. The bounce will attract retail bottom-fishers who will quickly be underwater when the second wave of selling hits. Pattern: gap down → 30-60 min bounce to ~$30-31 → continuation lower toward $25-27.
positions.macroContext: Risk-off day (VIX 27.62, S&P -0.53%, Nasdaq -0.77%, Iran war fears). Weak macro = extra headwind for any bounce attempts in beaten-down names. Financial/PE sector also weak (KKR -3.96%, APO -4.03%, ARES -4.79%).
positions.riskNote: Risk: If mystery catalyst is a buyout rumor or forced covering, this could snap back hard. That's why I'm NOT shorting the open — I need to see the bounce fail at resistance before committing. Half-size if conviction drops.
positions.notes: Do NOT short CNXC if: (a) price gaps back above $33 before my entry (breakdown invalidated), (b) positive news hits (deal, buyback, guidance raise), or (c) volume dries up completely on the bounce (no sellers = no fuel for leg 2 down). This is a one-day trade. Out by 3:30 PM CDT no matter what.

rules.eodRule: Must close all positions OR set hard stops by 3:45 PM CDT each day — this applies to BOTH longs AND shorts

shortRules.overnightShorts: NEVER hold a short overnight without a hard stop confirmed in the platform. If stop cannot be set, close before 3:45 PM CDT.
shortTriggers: Gap-up on weak or unconfirmed news — likely to fade when buyers exhaust
Overextended move (50%+ single-day spike) with no institutional backing
Technical breakdown below key support on high volume
Structural short: broken business model + no catalyst for recovery
Gap-DOWN breakdown (-10%+) followed by dead-cat bounce into resistance
Negative catalyst confirmed (earnings miss, guidance cut, FDA rejection, contract loss)
Risk-off macro environment (VIX elevated, broad market selling) amplifying individual weakness
Insider selling at current price levels (SEC Form 4 signals)
shortAvoid: Never short a stock with >20% short float (squeeze risk too high)
Never short into an earnings release or FDA decision (binary risk)
Never short a stock below $0.50 (circuit breaker rules, halt risk)
Never add to a losing short (no averaging down on shorts — they can go infinite)
Never short INTO a gap-down open (wait for the bounce before entering)
Never short without a defined stop set BEFORE entry
shortRules.profitLogic: On SHORT positions: profit when price FALLS, loss when price RISES. Entry price minus current price = P&L per share.
shortRules.positionMarking: All short positions must be marked type: 'SHORT' in the positions array.

bounceRules.description: For gap-down breakdown shorts, ALWAYS wait for the dead-cat bounce before entry
bounceRules.bounceWindow: Typically 10:00–11:30 AM CDT after a gap-down open
bounceRules.bounceTarget: Look for bounce to prior support-turned-resistance, often 50–60% retrace of the gap
bounceRules.entrySignal: Short when volume drops on the bounce AND price stalls at resistance (lower highs forming)
bounceRules.rationale: Shorting INTO a gap-down means chasing. The bounce gives a higher price entry, tighter stop, and better risk/reward.

grahamNote:
GRAHAM'S DAY TRADING STRATEGY — Updated March 24, 2026 (LONG + SHORT)

--- PHILOSOPHY ---
This account runs BOTH sides of the market. Long when momentum is with us, short when the setup calls for it. The edge is the same either way: setup quality, catalyst clarity, volume confirmation, and ironclad risk discipline. Direction doesn't matter — edge does.

I scan top gainers AND losers every morning before 9:30 AM. On the long side, I'm hunting momentum. On the short side, I'm hunting three things: (1) prior-day spike fades, (2) gap-down breakdowns with dead-cat bounce setups, and (3) structural bleeder names on any bounce.

--- MARKET CONTEXT: MARCH 24, 2026 ---
Risk-off day. VIX at 27.62 (elevated). S&P -0.53%, Nasdaq -0.77%. Iran war concerns in the news. Broad selling pressure = tailwind for shorts, headwind for longs. In this environment, I'm weighting toward shorts and holding longs with tighter stops.

--- LONG FRAMEWORK (unchanged) ---
  1. Real catalyst confirmed?
  2. Volume 3x+ average?
  3. Entry within 5% of intraday low (not chasing)?
  4. Stop defined before entry?
  5. Position ≤ 20%?
  6. Macro not working against me? (On red market days, longs need extra conviction)

--- SHORT FRAMEWORK ---
TWO TYPES of short setups I run:

TYPE 1 — SPIKE FADE (prior-day runner reversing)
  1. Is the gap-up catalyst WEAK or UNCONFIRMED?
  2. Is the move overextended (50%+ single day, thin float)?
  3. Did volume exhaust? (Volume declining = buyers gone)
  4. Short float < 20% (no squeeze trap)?
  5. Stop defined at entry +3%?

TYPE 2 — BREAKDOWN BOUNCE FADE (gap-down name, wait for bounce)
  1. Did the stock gap down hard (-10%+) on confirmed negative catalyst?
  2. Is the bounce stalling at prior support-now-resistance?
  3. Is bounce volume LOWER than the initial selloff volume?
  4. Does the macro environment support continued weakness?
  5. Stop defined (entry price +3%)?
  6. Do I have a clear intraday exit time (out by 3:30 PM)?

If any box is unchecked, I pass.

--- TODAY'S SHORT WATCHLIST (March 24, 2026) ---

🔴 CNXC SHORT — NEW (Bounce-Fade setup)
Concentrix gapped down -13.44% to $28.60 on heavy volume. Clear institutional distribution — this is NOT a dip to buy, it's a broken chart. I'll wait for the 10–11 AM dead-cat bounce toward $30.50–$31.50, then short the failure. ~32 shares, ~$976 position (19.5%). Hard stop at $32.50. Targets: $27.00, then $25.00.
WHY TODAY: Risk-off macro (VIX 27.62) provides extra selling pressure. BPO sector already facing AI headwinds. The bounce-fail pattern is textbook in high-VIX environments.
NOT entering if: bounces cleanly above $33 (breakdown invalidated), positive news hits, or volume disappears on the bounce (no fuel for leg 2).

🔴 BIAF SHORT (CONDITIONAL — fade the prior spike)
BioAffinity ran +73% on March 23 on unconfirmed catalyst. Classic fade setup. Only enter if the March 23 LONG is closed/stopped out AND price fails $4.00 by 9:50 AM. Half-size (~$480). Stop at entry +3%. Targets: $3.50, then $2.80.

🔴 CHGG SHORT (STRUCTURAL bleeder)
Chegg = broken AI-disrupted business. Watch for bounce to $0.65–0.75 on low volume, then short. ~1,400 shares (~$980). Hard stop $0.78. Targets: $0.50, $0.35. Out by 3:30 PM.

--- EXISTING LONGS ---
🛰️ SATL — Trailing stop up. Watch the $5.50 target. On a red market day, I'll tighten the stop — don't let a winner turn into a loser.
🧬 BIAF LONG — First order of business at open: is it holding or failing? Manage the long before considering the short flip.

--- RISK MANAGEMENT (LONG AND SHORT) ---
• Max 3% loss per position — no exceptions, long OR short
• Max 20% of $5,000 per trade ($1,000 ceiling)
• Never hold day trades overnight without a HARD STOP — especially shorts
• If down 6% on account in a single day: STOP TRADING. Walk away.
• Short-specific: NEVER add to a losing short. One bad add can wreck an account.
• Macro-aware: On high-VIX days, longs get smaller, shorts get more attention
• Profits stay in the account until Nick says otherwise

--- EDGE CHECKLIST (before EVERY trade) ---
[ ] Catalyst confirmed (or for shorts: weak/absent)?
[ ] Volume confirming the move?
[ ] Entry price is smart (not chasing; not too early on a bounce fade)?
[ ] Stop defined BEFORE entry?
[ ] Position size ≤ 20%?
[ ] No binary events in next 24h?
[ ] Macro environment supporting the direction?

All boxes checked? Execute with confidence. Any box unchecked? Pass.

Measure twice, cut once. Same rule whether I'm building a position or tearing one down.
